zh.lifeSummary: 當John Wood於大約1620年出生在英國時，他的父親John Wood32歲，母親Mrs John Wood32歲。他於大約1657年與Mary Peabody結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1673年在美國麻薩諸塞州約克比德福德紐托被列為deputy to general assembly,。他去世於1704年8月26日在美國羅得島紐波特米德萊托享年85歲，埋葬在美國羅得島紐波特米德萊托伍德 羅特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Wood于大约1620年出生在英国时，他的父亲John Wood32岁，母亲Mrs John Wood32岁。他于大约1657年与Mary Peabody结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1673年在美国马萨诸塞州約克比德福德紐托被列为deputy to general assembly,。他去世于1704年8月26日在美国罗德岛州紐波特米德萊托享年85岁，埋葬在美国罗德岛州紐波特米德萊托伍德 羅特。

nameMeanings.name: Wood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: mainly a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a wood, from Middle English wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as French Bois and Polish Les .
English: in a few cases, a nickname for an eccentric or perhaps a violent person, from Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd).

variants.name: Woode
meanings: English: variant of   Wood  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 312, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Woods
meanings: English: variant of Wood with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish: adopted as a translation of Ó Cuill ‘descendant of Coll’ (see Quill ), or in Ulster of Mac Con Coille ‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’, which has also been mistranslated Cox , as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Historical Boundaries: 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

The Colony of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations, as it was from June 22, 1703, when it created the counties of Providence Plantations and Rhode Island, to June 16, 1729, when Providence Plantations County was renamed Providence, Rhode Island County was renamed Newport, and Kings County was created from Providence.

en.lifeSummary: When John Wood was born about 1620, in England, his father, John Wood, was 32 and his mother, Mrs John Wood, was 32. He married Mary Peabody about 1657. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. In 1673, his occupation is listed as deputy to general assembly, in Newtown, Biddeford, York, Massachusetts, United States. He died on 26 August 1704, in Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, United States, at the age of 85, and was buried in Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Wood est né environ 1620, à Angleterre, son père, John Wood, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs John Wood, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Mary Peabody environ 1657. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. En 1673, sa profession de son indiquée est deputy to general assembly, à Newtown, Biddeford, York, Massachusetts, États-Unis. Il est décédé le 26 août 1704, à Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, États-Unis, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Wood um 1620 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Wood 32 und seine Mutter Mrs John Wood 32. Er hat um 1657 Mary Peabody geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1673 ist sein Beruf als deputy to general assembly, in Newtown, Biddeford, York, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. August 1704 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Wood è nato il circa 1620 nel England, il suo padre, John Wood, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Mrs John Wood, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Mary Peabody circa 1657. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1673, la sua occupazione è indicata come deputy to general assembly, nel Newtown, Biddeford, York, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America. Morì il 26 agosto 1704, nel Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolto a Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: John Woodが約1620年にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Woodは32歳，母Mrs John Woodは32歳であった。約1657年にMary Peabodyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1673年，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ヨークビッドエフォードニュートウンでの職業はdeputy to general assembly,と記録されている。彼は1704年8月26日にアメリカ合衆国ロードアイランド州ニュープオルトミッドレトウンで85歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ロードアイランド州ニュープオルトミッドレトウンウッド ロトにて埋葬された。

ko.Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Isl: 미국 로드아일랜드 주 뉴포트 미들레타운 우드 로트
ko.lifeSummary: John Wood(이)가 대략 1620년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Wood(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs John Wood(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1657년 Mary Peabody(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1673년, 그의 직업은 미국 매사추세츠 주 요르크 비데퍼드 뉴타운에서 deputy to general assembly,이었다. 그는 1704년 8월 26일 미국 로드아일랜드 주 뉴포트 미들레타운에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 로드아일랜드 주 뉴포트 미들레타운 우드 로트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Wood nasceu aproximadamente 1620, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Wood, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Mrs John Wood, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Peabody aproximadamente 1657. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1673, sua profissão aparece como deputy to general assembly, em Newtown, Biddeford, York, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América. Ele faleceu em 26 de agosto de 1704, em Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Estados Unidos da América, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Wood родился около 1620 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Wood было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs John Wood, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Peabody около 1657 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. В 1673 году он работал по профессии deputy to general assembly, в местности нютоун, биддефорд, ерк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты. Он умер 26 августа 1704 г. в местности миддлетоун, нюпорт, Род-Айленд, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности вуд лот, миддлетоун, нюпорт, Род-Айленд, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Wood nació aproximadamente 1620, en Inglaterra, su papá, John Wood, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mrs John Wood, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Mary Peabody aproximadamente 1657. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. En 1673, su ocupación registrada era deputy to general assembly, en Newtown, Biddeford, York, Masachusets, Estados Unidos. Murió el 26 de agosto de 1704, en Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Wood Lot, Middletown, Newport, Rhode Island, Estados Unidos.
